steps.title: Identify the core contrast
steps.content: The passage opens with acknowledgment of motor control having “hindered recognition of other possible functions,” then pivots with “now suggests” to reveal new discoveries about emotion, social behavior, and reward processing. The key signal is this before-and-after structure.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: C correctly identifies the change in scientific understanding: the cerebellum, long thought to control motor functions, is now recognized to have additional roles in emotion and social behavior. A frames the new findings as confirmatory rather than revelatory; B narrowly focuses on VTA pathway claims instead of the cerebellum's broader scope; D fabricates a technological cause. This matches choice C — C is correct.
